name: Jauan Jennings
headline: Proven slot grinder buried in a crowded Vikings room
body:
Jennings is a more capable receiver than his new depth chart suggests. In 2024 he posted a 22% target share, a 0.503 WOPR, and 2.49 yards per route run on 113 looks, then followed it with a 9-touchdown 2025 on a 16.4% share despite a dip to 1.47 yards per route. The red-zone profile travels: 19 red-zone and 8 end-zone targets last year explain those scores, and a 3.3% drop rate shows reliable hands. His efficiency cratered, though, with EPA per play sliding from 0.383 to -0.016 as the volume and air yards thinned.

The move to Minnesota caps the upside hard. Slotted third behind Justin Jefferson and Jordan Addison, plus Hockenson and a new quarterback in Kyler Murray, he projects for roughly 49 catches and 557 yards. That puts him in late-round, touchdown-dependent territory: a WR60-ish flier whose value spikes only if an injury opens snaps ahead of him.
